task.title: Report the first missing version for wider append gaps
task.objective: Complete issue https://github.com/Knosence9/project-vela/issues/414 by making append-side VersionGap diagnostics report the first missing stream version for gaps wider than one version while preserving pre-encoding rejection, error precedence, and atomic revalidation.
acceptance_criteria: Stored stream versions one and four produce EventLogError::VersionGap with expected version two and found version four.
The invalid append is rejected before payload encoding and writes no event.
Existing invalid-version, exhausted-stream, contiguous append, and replay behavior remains intact.
The stable error contract describes the first missing version and the first stored version observed after the gap.
Pinned local verification, the Rust quality gate, CodeRabbit review, and squash auto-merge pass.

attempts.summary: Widened the append continuity regression from stored versions one and three to versions one and four while retaining the serializer-that-must-not-run and unchanged-row-count assertions.
attempts.diagnostic: The RED test received 'stored stream version gap: expected version 3, found 4' instead of expected version 2, proving that deriving expected as found minus one reports the immediate predecessor rather than the first missing version for a wider gap.
attempts.patch: Changed only the regression fixture and assertions before running the focused test.

attempts.summary: Derived the first missing version from the stored prefix preceding the first observed row without an immediate predecessor.
attempts.diagnostic: The focused regression passed with VersionGap { expected: 2, found: 4 }; serialization did not run and the stored row count remained two.
attempts.patch: Used one SQLite CTE query to retain minimum, maximum, first-gap, and terminal-version precedence while computing the first missing version as the greatest earlier stored version plus one, or one when no earlier row exists.

attempts.summary: Ran the complete pinned verification and reviewed the bounded diff before commit.
attempts.diagnostic: All 25 event-log integration tests, workspace tests, formatting, checking, Clippy with warnings denied, SecretSpec integration, and diff checks passed; the read-only diff review found no blocking correctness issue across empty, contiguous, corrupt, gapped, and terminal streams.
attempts.patch: No production correction was required after GREEN; documentation was updated with wider-gap semantics.

attempts.summary: Addressed the only CodeRabbit review finding and repeated full verification.
attempts.diagnostic: CodeRabbit identified awkward wording in the stable error contract and requested 'the first stored version observed after the gap'; the wording was corrected, the thread was resolved with commit 1628ff1, and just verify passed again.
attempts.patch: Changed one documentation sentence without altering behavior.

attempts.summary: Completed required pull-request checks and squash auto-merge.
attempts.diagnostic: Rust quality gate runs 29341468421 and 29341828091 passed, CodeRabbit passed after the review correction, and pull request 415 squash-merged as b07513d5.

outcome.summary: Append now reports the first missing stream version for wider stored gaps before caller serialization and still repeats validation inside the immediate transaction; the public diagnostic and documentation agree.

verification.command: nix develop --command cargo test -p vela-kernel --test event_log reports_the_first_missing_version_for_a_wider_gap_before_encoding -- --exact # RED: expected version 3 was reported instead of 2
nix develop --command cargo test -p vela-kernel --test event_log reports_the_first_missing_version_for_a_wider_gap_before_encoding -- --exact # GREEN
nix develop --command cargo test -p vela-kernel --test event_log before_encoding
nix develop --command just verify before pull request 415
Independent read-only pre-commit review of the issue 414 diff: no blocking findings
GitHub Rust quality gate run 29341468421 for pull request 415
CodeRabbit review for pull request 415: one documentation wording finding fixed and resolved in 1628ff1
nix develop --command just verify after the review correction
GitHub Rust quality gate run 29341828091 for pull request 415
Required CodeRabbit status after review correction for pull request 415
nix develop --command cargo run --locked -p vela-dev -- record validate corpus/development/0023-first-missing-append-gap.json # result: valid development record
nix develop --command cargo run --locked -p vela-dev -- corpus inspect corpus/development # result: 23 valid, 0 invalid
nix develop --command just verify with development record 0023

lessons: The first stored row lacking an immediate predecessor identifies where a gap ends, but subtracting one from that row does not identify where a wider gap begins.
For an ordered unique stream, the first missing version is one greater than the greatest stored version before the first post-gap row, or one when the stream starts above one.
A wider-gap regression distinguishes a true continuity diagnostic from an implementation that only works when exactly one version is missing.
Keeping minimum, maximum, first-missing, and first-found values in one SQLite statement preserves a consistent snapshot for both unlocked preflight and transactional revalidation.
A serializer that must not run and an unchanged row-count assertion jointly pin validation order and storage non-mutation.
Automated review can improve the precision of a public error contract even when the behavior and tests are already correct.
